name: Coll de l'Ordal and Olesa de Bonesvalls Loop
name_original: Avinyó Nou y Olesa de Bonesvalls – vuelta por el Parque de Garraf
description: This moderate 22.6-mile road cycling route through Garraf Park offers challenging climbs, Mediterranean views, and diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Hiking around Avinyonet Del Penedès offers a diverse landscape combining expansive vineyards with mountainous, forested regions. The region features relatively flat plains dominated by vineyards, providing scenic routes. Contrasting with the plains, there are also mountainous areas with abundant forests, creating varied ecosystems. Several points of interest provide panoramic views of the local vineyards and the surrounding natural features. Best hiking trails around Avinyonet Del Penedès The most popular hiking route is Ermitas de Sant Pere de Lavern y Sant Joan Salerm — circular desde Sant Sadurní d'Anoia, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete. This circular path connects two local hermitages through the countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is From Avinyó Nou to Sant Pau d'Ordal — circular by the Mirador de les Gunyoles (Colina Mireta), a moderate 8.8 miles (14.1 km) path. This route offers views from the Mirador de les Gunyoles. Local hikers also love the From Sant Pau d'Ordal to the Colina de las Mentides — circular through the Sierra de Crestabocs, a 7.2 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Crestabocs, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Avinyonet Del Penedès is defined by expansive vineyards, forested hills, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Avinyonet Del Penedès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 5,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Avinyonet Del Penedès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Avinyonet Del Penedès?
A: Avinyonet Del Penedès offers a wide variety of hiking trails, with nearly 400 routes available. These include over 110 easy trails, more than 250 moderate routes, and around 20 difficult options, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Avinyonet Del Penedès?
A: The region boasts a diverse landscape, combining expansive vineyards with mountainous, forested areas. You'll find relatively flat plains dominated by vineyards, offering scenic and gentle routes, alongside more challenging mountainous terrain with rich forests. This provides a varied experience, from open agricultural views to shaded woodland paths.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Avinyonet Del Penedès has over 110 easy trails. For a relaxed walk, consider routes like the 'L'Origen de la Vinya Trail' (3.5 km) or the 'Santa Magdalena Trail' (6 km), which are well-suited for beginners or families looking for a gentle outing.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Avinyonet Del Penedès?
A: Yes, many trails in the area are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356829570/ermitas-de-sant-pere-de-lavern-y-sant-joan-salerm-circular-desde-sant-sadurni-d-anoia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermitas de Sant Pere de Lavern y Sant Joan Salerm — circular desde Sant Sadurní d'Anoia&lt;/a&gt;, a 9 km path connecting two local hermitages. Another is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357141893/from-avinyo-nou-to-sant-pau-d-ordal-circular-by-the-mirador-de-les-gunyoles-colina-mireta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Avinyó Nou to Sant Pau d'Ordal — circular by the Mirador de les Gunyoles (Colina Mireta)&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views.

Q: What cultural or historical sites can I explore on a hike?
A: Hiking paths in Avinyonet Del Penedès often lead to significant cultural and historical sites. You can visit the Iberian archaeological site of 'Turó de la Font de la Canya,' the Romanesque monastery of Sant Sebastià dels Gorgs, or the medieval dovecote and tower of l'Arboçar, which offers magnificent vineyard views. The Roman tower and old quarter of Les Gunyoles are also points of interest.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views during my hike?
A: Several points offer breathtaking vistas. 'El Circell' is a lookout point with 360-degree views of the local vineyards. Other significant viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2724790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morella Summit&lt;/a&gt;, providing views from the Parc del Garraf into Barcelona, and the Rat Penat Climb, which offers views of the Garraf coast and Barcelona city. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2185904" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gavà - Begues Climb&lt;/a&gt; also provides impressive views.

Q: Are there any long-distance or more challenging trails?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, the region offers routes like 'Les Serres Trail' (17 km, Very difficult) and 'La Serra de la Llampa Trail'. There are approximately 20 difficult routes in total, providing options for experienced hikers.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Avinyonet Del Penedès?
A: The trails in Avinyonet Del Penedès are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic vineyard views, and the well-maintained paths that lead through both natural landscapes and charming towns.

Q: Is Avinyonet Del Penedès suitable for dog-friendly hiking?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, the natural and varied landscape of Avinyonet Del Penedès, with its numerous forest tracks and paths, generally offers many opportunities for hiking with dogs. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash and respect local signage.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Avinyonet Del Penedès?
A: The region is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with blooming vineyards or rich autumnal colors. Even in winter, the climate can be mild, making it suitable for walks, while summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Are there any notable attractions near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, you can find several attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Buddhist Monastery, Sakya Tashi Ling&lt;/a&gt;, is a unique cultural site. You can also explore various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4048004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt de les Bassioles&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2254768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de l'Ordal&lt;/a&gt;, which offer additional points of interest and scenic drives.

Q: Are there options for public transport or parking near the trailheads?
A: Avinyonet Del Penedès is accessible, and many trailheads are located near towns or villages. While specific public transport options to every trailhead may vary, local bus services connect the main towns. Parking is generally available in or near the villages that serve as starting points for many routes. It's recommended to check specific route details for precise parking information.
tips: Romanesque monastery founded in 1030 in the old pre-Romanesque chapel of Els Gorgs, to which a bell tower was added during its reform. Between 1981 and 1977 the cloister was restored, recovering its original appearance, and in a second phase, between 2000 and 2002, the bell tower was restored. A third restoration between 2010 and 2011 protected the cloister with a new cover.
Nice and hard port on its final ramp. The bad thing is that it has quite fast traffic

This is one of my favourite stretches in the Garraf area. It is a very winding road in perfect condition, with a very irregular profile, with some very steep slopes, which crosses a pine forest and several housing estates until it reaches the top. The descent to Vallivana is difficult, especially due to the steep slope.
It is a highly recommended area on cool days from November to April.
Beautiful highway. Watch out for curves.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling experience with varied and challenging terrain. You'll encounter winding roads, generally in excellent condition, but be prepared for an irregular profile that includes some very steep asphalt climbs. While mostly paved, some segments within the broader Garraf Park area might feature unpaved or gravel sections, which could be difficult for road bikes.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery. You can expect fantastic panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea during initial ascents. As you delve into Garraf Park, the landscape transitions to a barren hinterland, pine forests, and various housing estates. Notable points include the Monastery of Saint Sebastian of the Gorgs, Coll de l'Ordal, and the Olesa de Bonesvalls to Vallirana Scenic Road.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: It is particularly recommended to cycle this route during the cooler months, from November to April, to avoid the intense summer heat. The moderate temperatures make the challenging climbs more enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route aren't detailed, Avinyó Nou and Olesa de Bonesvalls are common starting points for cyclists in the area. You would typically look for public parking within these towns.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as moderate and requires good fitness due to its significant elevation changes and steep slopes. It is best suited for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenging ride rather than beginners.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits to cycle in Garraf Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, no special permits are required for road cycling on established routes within Garraf Park. However, always respect local regulations and signage, especially concerning protected areas.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in Garraf Park, bringing a dog on a racebike tour of this difficulty and terrain is not typically recommended. If you do, ensure your dog is safely secured and able to handle the pace and distance, and always keep them on a leash where required.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Avinyó Nou and Olesa de Bonesvalls are towns along the route where you can find cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options. These towns serve as good spots for a break or to extend your stay.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Penedès 360 Slow Bike (15% of the route), Camí Ral de Barcelona a Vilafranca (14%), and Penedès 360 Mountain Bike (ruta 5 dies) (12%).

faqs.question: What kind of challenge should I expect on this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Expect a moderate to hard challenge. The route features an irregular profile with significant elevation gain (630 meters) over 36.3 kilometers, including very steep asphalt climbs. Your effort will be rewarded with spectacular Mediterranean vistas.

faqs.question: Are there any sections with unpaved roads that might be difficult for a racebike?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, some segments within the broader Garraf Park area may feature unpaved or gravel sections. These could pose difficulties for traditional road bikes, so be prepared for varied surfaces.
